description: In Rust, prefer established idioms (ownership and borrowing, Result/Option with ?, and RAII via Drop) over generic rewrites.
body: In Rust, the ecosystem has settled idioms — ownership and borrowing, Result/Option with ?, and RAII via Drop — and changes that ignore them add risk and review friction. Use when adding or modifying Rust code near existing patterns. Do not impose a foreign style or rewrite working code into a generic shape, and avoid abandoning nearby conventions. Actions: read the surrounding code, follow its ownership and borrowing, Result/Option with ?, and RAII via Drop, keep the change minimal, then run cargo clippy -D warnings and rustc lint output and cargo test on the affected crate with the relevant features to confirm fit. Validation signals include a small diff consistent with neighbors, a clean lint pass, and passing tests. Risk boundary: a broad idiom migration is its own reviewed task; escalate rather than bundling it into an unrelated change.
